verses.text: God speaks: 'I am the LORD thy God, which... brought thee out of... bondage.' Redeemer first. The law rests on grace already given.
God speaks: 'I am the LORD thy God, which... brought thee out of... bondage.' Redeemer first. The law rests on grace already given.
No other gods, no graven image; He is 'a jealous God,' showing mercy to those who love Him. First commands. God alone is to be worshipped.
No other gods, no graven image; He is 'a jealous God,' showing mercy to those who love Him. First commands. God alone is to be worshipped.
No other gods, no graven image; He is 'a jealous God,' showing mercy to those who love Him. First commands. God alone is to be worshipped.
No other gods, no graven image; He is 'a jealous God,' showing mercy to those who love Him. First commands. God alone is to be worshipped.
Take not His name in vain. Reverence. God's name must be held holy.
Remember the sabbath day to keep it holy, as God rested the seventh day. Rest. One day in seven is hallowed to God.
Remember the sabbath day to keep it holy, as God rested the seventh day. Rest. One day in seven is hallowed to God.
Remember the sabbath day to keep it holy, as God rested the seventh day. Rest. One day in seven is hallowed to God.
Remember the sabbath day to keep it holy, as God rested the seventh day. Rest. One day in seven is hallowed to God.
Honour thy father and mother, that thy days be long. Honour. The first command with a promise attached.
Kill not; commit not adultery; steal not. Neighbour-love. God guards life, marriage, and property.
Kill not; commit not adultery; steal not. Neighbour-love. God guards life, marriage, and property.
Kill not; commit not adultery; steal not. Neighbour-love. God guards life, marriage, and property.
Bear no false witness; covet nothing of thy neighbour's. Heart and tongue. The law reaches even to desire.
Bear no false witness; covet nothing of thy neighbour's. Heart and tongue. The law reaches even to desire.
The people, terrified, beg Moses to speak; he says God proves them, that they fear and sin not. Trembling. Holy fear keeps from sin.
The people, terrified, beg Moses to speak; he says God proves them, that they fear and sin not. Trembling. Holy fear keeps from sin.
The people, terrified, beg Moses to speak; he says God proves them, that they fear and sin not. Trembling. Holy fear keeps from sin.
The people, terrified, beg Moses to speak; he says God proves them, that they fear and sin not. Trembling. Holy fear keeps from sin.
God forbids gods of silver and gold; an altar of earth or unhewn stone. Plain worship. God wants the heart, not man's adornment.
